files.name: Learning-JavaScript
Algorithms
files.content:
# Understanding Algorithms

## What are Algorithms?

An algorithm is a step-by-step procedure for solving a problem or accomplishing a task.

### Key Characteristics
- **Definite** - Clear and unambiguous steps
- **Finite** - Must terminate in finite time
- **Effective** - Produces correct results
- **Efficient** - Optimized for time and space

## Common Algorithm Types

### Sorting Algorithms
- Bubble Sort - O(n²)
- Quick Sort - O(n log n)
- Merge Sort - O(n log n)
- Heap Sort - O(n log n)

### Searching Algorithms
- Linear Search - O(n)
- Binary Search - O(log n)
- Breadth-First Search (BFS)
- Depth-First Search (DFS)

### Graph Algorithms
- Dijkstra's - Shortest path
- Kruskal's - Minimum spanning tree
- Floyd-Warshall - All pairs shortest path

## Complexity Analysis

### Big O Notation

| Notation | Name | Example |
|----------|------|---------|
| O(1) | Constant | Array indexing |
| O(log n) | Logarithmic | Binary search |
| O(n) | Linear | Simple loop |
| O(n log n) | Linearithmic | Merge sort |
| O(n²) | Quadratic | Nested loops |
| O(2ⁿ) | Exponential | Recursive fibonacci |

## Practice Problems

- [ ] Implement quicksort
- [ ] Solve LeetCode medium problems
- [ ] Understand dynamic programming
- [ ] Master graph algorithms

## Resources

- [Big O Cheatsheet](https://www.bigocheatsheet.com/)
- Cracking the Coding Interview
- LeetCode algorithms section

files.name: Portfolio-Project
files.content:
# Portfolio Project

Building my personal portfolio website with Next.js and React.

## Objectives

1. Showcase my projects and experience
2. Create an Obsidian-like vault viewer
3. Implement interactive components
4. Optimize for performance

## Architecture

```
portfolio/
├── app/          - Next.js app directory
├── components/   - React components
├── lib/          - Utility functions
├── public/       - Static assets & vault
└── styles/       - CSS styles
```

## Stack

- **Frontend:** React 18, Next.js 14
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS
- **Animations:** Framer Motion
- **Icons:** Lucide React

## Current Status

- [x] Basic portfolio structure
- [x] Hero section
- [x] Experience section
- [x] Projects section
- [x] My Vault integration
- [ ] Blog section
- [ ] Comments system
- [ ] Dark mode improvements

## Next Tasks

1. Deploy to Vercel
2. Add blog section
3. Optimize images
4. Add SEO improvements

## Related Notes

- [[Learning JavaScript]] - Frontend development
- [[Web Development]] - General web concepts

files.name: CDC-Pipeline
files.content:
# Sample CDC Pipeline

A Change Data Capture (CDC) implementation for streaming database changes.

## Overview

This project captures changes from a source database and publishes them as events to a message broker.

## Architecture

```
Source DB → CDC Connector → Event Formatter → Kafka Topic → Consumer Applications
```

## Key Components

### 1. CDC Connector
- Polls database transaction logs
- Detects INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE operations
- Maintains LSN/SCN tracking

### 2. Event Formatter
- Converts database changes to JSON events
- Adds metadata (timestamp, operation type)
- Implements event schema

### 3. Kafka Producer
- Publishes events to Kafka topics
- Ensures idempotency
- Handles backpressure

### 4. Consumer Applications
- Subscribe to topics
- Process events in order
- Update local state

## Technologies

- Java 11+
- Kafka/Confluent Platform
- PostgreSQL/MySQL
- Spring Boot

## Setup

See [[Portfolio Project]] for setup instructions.

## Status

- [x] Initial implementation
- [x] Error handling
- [ ] Performance optimization
- [ ] Monitoring and alerting

files.name: README
files.content:
# Welcome to My Vault 🧠

This is my personal digital knowledge management system - my second brain. Here you'll find:

- **Notes** - My learning journey and knowledge base
- **Projects** - Project-specific documentation
- **Excalidraw** - Visual diagrams and sketches
- And more...

## Quick Navigation

- Explore the file tree on the left
- Search for notes using the search bar
- Click on tags to filter by topic
- View markdown content with full formatting

## Getting Started

1. Add your own notes to the vault folder
2. Use tags in your frontmatter for better organization
3. Link notes together with `[[wiki links]]`
4. Enjoy your knowledge graph! 📚

---

**Last Updated:** 2026-06-16
